CoinbaseAccount: Account, Balance, and Fee Information

Source: R/CoinbaseAccount.R
CoinbaseAccount.Rd

Retrieves authenticated account data from the Coinbase Advanced Trade API: trading accounts (balances), the transaction/fee summary, portfolios, and the API key's permissions. All endpoints require credentials.

Inherits from CoinbaseBase. All methods support both synchronous and asynchronous execution depending on the async argument at construction.

Pagination

get_accounts() walks Coinbase's body-cursor pagination (cursor / has_next) to return all accounts across pages.

Endpoints Covered

MethodEndpointAuth
get_accountsGET /api/v3/brokerage/accountsYes
get_accountGET /api/v3/brokerage/accounts/{uuid}Yes
get_feesGET /api/v3/brokerage/transaction_summaryYes
get_portfoliosGET /api/v3/brokerage/portfoliosYes
get_portfolio_breakdownGET /api/v3/brokerage/portfolios/{uuid}Yes
get_key_permissionsGET /api/v3/brokerage/key_permissionsYes

Super class

CoinbaseBase -> CoinbaseAccount

Methods

Public methods

  • CoinbaseAccount$get_accounts()

  • CoinbaseAccount$get_account()

  • CoinbaseAccount$get_fees()

  • CoinbaseAccount$get_portfolios()

  • CoinbaseAccount$get_portfolio_breakdown()

  • CoinbaseAccount$get_key_permissions()

  • CoinbaseAccount$clone()

Inherited methods
  • CoinbaseBase$initialize()

CoinbaseAccount$get_accounts()

Retrieve all trading accounts (balances), paginating over the cursor until exhausted.

Usage

CoinbaseAccount$get_accounts(limit = NULL, max_pages = Inf)

Arguments

limit

Integer or NULL; page size. Optional.

max_pages

Numeric; cap on pages fetched. Default Inf.

Returns

A data.table::data.table of accounts, or a promise thereof.

CoinbaseAccount$get_account()

Retrieve a single account by its UUID.

Usage

CoinbaseAccount$get_account(account_uuid)

Arguments

account_uuid

Character; the account UUID.

Returns

A single-row data.table::data.table, or a promise thereof.

CoinbaseAccount$get_fees()

Retrieve the transaction/fee summary, including the current maker/taker fee tier.

Usage

CoinbaseAccount$get_fees(product_type = NULL)

Arguments

product_type

Character or NULL; "SPOT" or "FUTURE" to scope the summary. Optional.

Returns

A single-row data.table::data.table, or a promise thereof.

CoinbaseAccount$get_portfolios()

Retrieve the user's portfolios.

Usage

CoinbaseAccount$get_portfolios()

Returns

A data.table::data.table of portfolios, or a promise thereof.

CoinbaseAccount$get_portfolio_breakdown()

Retrieve the detailed breakdown of a single portfolio: its spot, futures, and perpetual positions. Positions are returned as the data.table (one row per holding, with a position_type column); the portfolio's aggregate balance totals are attached as a one-row data.table::data.table in attr(x, "summary") (mirroring the "failures" attribute on coinbase_backfill_trades()).

Usage

CoinbaseAccount$get_portfolio_breakdown(portfolio_uuid, currency = NULL)

Arguments

portfolio_uuid

Character; the portfolio UUID (from get_portfolios()).

currency

Character or NULL; quote currency for fiat values. Optional.

Returns

A data.table::data.table of positions carrying a "summary" attribute, or a promise thereof.

CoinbaseAccount$get_key_permissions()

Retrieve the calling API key's permissions.

Usage

CoinbaseAccount$get_key_permissions()

Returns

A single-row data.table::data.table, or a promise thereof.

CoinbaseAccount$clone()

The objects of this class are cloneable with this method.

Usage

CoinbaseAccount$clone(deep = FALSE)

Arguments

deep

Whether to make a deep clone.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
account <- CoinbaseAccount$new()
account$get_accounts()
account$get_fees()
} # }
